sources.name: statisticsCatalog
sources.permission: Metadata visibility on the statistics object

privacy.runtimeFixtures: Synthetic Example* objects, users, replica names, and numeric values only

falsePositiveBoundaries: ReplicaRoleId, ReplicaRoleDesc, and ReplicaName identify the origin or last update ownership of a statistics object; they do not identify the current connection role or prove that a query used the statistics.
CurrentReplicaRole describes the database role at collection time and is intentionally separate from the statistics origin.
A later statistics update on the primary transfers ownership metadata to the primary and does not prove that the statistics were never created on a secondary.
Temporary and persisted permanent versions can coexist until recompilation or restart; duplicate column coverage is not by itself a defect.
ReplicaName is expected to be null for primary-origin statistics.
An empty result can mean an empty filter scope or restricted metadata visibility and is reported as limited evidence, not feature absence.

runtimeMatrix.availabilityGroupBoundary: The three-version container gate validates the real SQL Server 2025 catalog path and synthetic role semantics. It does not claim an active readable-secondary topology.
runtimeMatrix.fixtures: Synthetic Example* objects, users, replica names, and numeric values only
